mission.title: Install and Configure Redis Operator (Spotahome / OT-CONTAINER-KIT)
mission.description: The Redis Operator by OT-CONTAINER-KIT simplifies the deployment and management of Redis instances on Kubernetes, supporting standalone, cluster, and replication modes. It provides built-in monitoring and adheres to best practices for security and performance. This operator is ideal for teams looking to manage Redis workloads efficiently in a Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redis Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redis-operator https://ot-container-kit.github.io/redis-operator

helm repo update

```

> This adds the repository for the Redis Operator.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Redis Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Redis Operator in the 'redis-operator' namespace, creating the namespace if it does not exist.

```bash

kubectl create namespace redis-operator

helm install redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis-operator --version 0.19.0

```

> Creates the namespace for the Redis Operator.
> Installs the Redis Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the Redis Operator pods are running successfully.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n redis-operator

```

> Lists all pods in the redis-operator namespace to verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a Redis Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Redis cluster using a Custom Resource (CR) definition.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  size: 3
  redis:
    image: redis:7.0.0
EOF

```

> Creates a Redis cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  size: 3
  redis:
    image: redis:7.0.0
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Redis Cluster
steps.description:
This command checks if the Redis cluster is up and running.

```bash

kubectl get redis -n redis-operator

```

> Lists all Redis instances in the redis-operator namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Redis Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redis Operator and all associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss if persistent volumes are not managed properly.

```bash

helm uninstall redis-operator --namespace redis-operator

kubectl delete namespace redis-operator

```

> Uninstalls the Redis Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Redis Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your Redis data. This step will upgrade the Redis Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get redis -n redis-operator -o yaml > redis-backup.yaml

helm upgrade redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis-operator --version 0.19.0

kubectl get pods -n redis-operator

```

> Backup existing Redis configurations.
> Upgrades the Redis Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the upgraded pods are running.
commands: kubectl get redis -n redis-operator -o yaml > redis-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Redis instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or misconfigured RBAC.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service account used by the operator has the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redis instance not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Redis pods are not starting due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n redis-operator' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Redis'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate network issues or that the Redis service is not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure the Redis pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redis cluster is not healthy'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if one or more Redis nodes are down.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Redis nodes and restart any that are not running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Redis 7.0, improved monitoring capabilities with redis-exporter.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Redis instance listed with 'kubectl get redis -n redis-operator', showing its status as 'Ready'.

metadata:

  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  size: 3
  redis:
    image: redis:7.0.0
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
